page: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dalv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads. It includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low-memory ratio of 2.0 GB per core. No local storage is bundled, though the instance is premium storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 3078.57 and Geekbench single-core scoring 2022.0, both placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core and parallelized workloads show significant limitations, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only small block and large block bandwidths categorized as weak, though large block write bandwidth remains average at 27.76 GB/sec.

Given the high single-core clock speed but limited 4.0 GB memory capacity and weak multi-core scaling, this instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded tasks. It performs adequately on single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, as well as text processing. However, it is poorly suited for database operations, software compilation, or heavy multi-threaded workloads, as evidenced by a poor PassMark database operations score in the bottom 10% and weak Redis SET performance. It represents a cost-efficient entry point for dedicated compute resources where memory and multi-core throughput are not primary requirements.
description: The Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a dedicated x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 2 vCPUs and a low-memory configuration of 4.0 GB RAM, with no bundled local storage or GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance, memory read bandwidth, and database operations are weak, with PassMark database operations falling into the bottom 10%. This resource profile makes the D2als_v6 highly cost-efficient for lightweight, single-threaded workloads such as basic text processing, single-threaded compression, or simple web serving, while rendering it unsuitable for memory-intensive databases or parallelized compilation tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a dedicated virtual machine featuring a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, 2 vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory capacity.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D2als_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with 4.0 GB RAM, powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC processor, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU instance offering strong single-core AMD EPYC performance with a compact, cost-efficient four-gigabyte memory footprint.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Configured with 4.0 GB of system memory and no local storage
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance in the top 25%
Exhibits weak multi-core scaling and constrained memory read bandwidth
Best suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and basic text processing
Not recommended for database operations or parallelized software compilation
